pattern: Sorting the array descending turns 'exactly x elements are at least x' into a single boundary check at position x, since the sorted order makes the count of qualifying elements monotonic in a way that's easy to test directly.
hints: There are at most n+1 candidate values worth checking for x: 0 through n, since counting how many elements are >= x can never distinguish between finer values.
For a candidate x, count how many elements in nums are at least x; x is valid exactly when that count equals x itself.
Sorting descending helps: if the x-th largest element (1-indexed) is >= x, and the (x+1)-th largest (if it exists) is < x, then exactly x elements qualify.

edge_inputs.description: a straightforward case with a unique valid x
no valid x exists for this array
multiple values tie right at the boundary, tempting an off-by-one
all zeros with several elements, must return zero achievability correctly
large values with only a couple of elements

stress.description: random arrays across the constraint range
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = max(1, min(size, 100))
    nums = [rng.randint(0, 1000) for _ in range(n)]
    return [nums]

complexity.time: O(n^2) or O(n log n) sorted
